items.title: Purpose
items.body: A lightweight design-system contract for the Burgilau landing page and calculator experience. Token values are defined in design-system/tokens.json and exported to src/scss/abstracts/_variables.scss.

items.guidance: Use semantic CSS custom properties instead of hard-coded visual values in components.
items.rationale: Semantic tokens keep visual changes centralized and make theming possible without rewriting component styles.

items.guidance: Keep normal and interactive text/background combinations at WCAG AA contrast or better.
items.rationale: The landing page is task-focused and must remain readable on mobile and desktop.

entities.description: Semantic color tokens used by the page, calculator, cards, buttons, and focus states.

items.guidance: Name color tokens by semantic role and state: background-*, text-*, border-*, and icon-*.
items.rationale: Role-based semantic tokens scale across components and keep values independent from implementation details.

items.guidance: Use --color-border-focus for shared visible keyboard focus indicators.
items.rationale: A single focus border token makes accessibility checks and future theme changes predictable.

items.guidance: Use --color-background-accent and --color-border-accent only for primary actions, WhatsApp-related action surfaces, and emphasized states.
items.rationale: Accent roles carry conversion weight and should not be diluted by decorative use.

entities.description: Spacing scale and page layout spacing tokens.

items.guidance: Use spacing tokens for component padding, gaps, and layout rhythm.
items.rationale: Consistent spacing keeps the compact mobile-first layout predictable.

entities.description: Radius tokens for page sections, cards, and buttons.

items.guidance: Use the most specific radius token for the surface being styled.
items.rationale: Section, card, and button surfaces have different visual weights.

entities.name: Button
entities.description: Primary action control used for conversion-oriented calls to action.

items.description: Starting a WhatsApp contact flow or confirming the main page action.
Uses the accent background hover token where the button is accent-backed.
Shows a visible focus indicator using the shared focus token.

entities.name: Option Card
entities.description: Selectable card presentation for calculator radio options.

items.guidance: Expose native radio focus on the visible option card label.
items.rationale: Keyboard users must not lose the active focus location when radios are visually hidden.

items.description: Uses the shared hover border token.
Uses the shared selected surface background token.
Shows a visible focus indicator through the associated native radio.

entities.name: Calculator
entities.description: Configuration-driven price calculator that renders controls from resolved service definitions.

dataAttributes.description: Stable service identifier used to resolve calculator configuration.
Container where generated parameter controls are rendered.

items.guidance: Render calculator options from resolved service configuration instead of hard-coding option groups in HTML.
items.rationale: The calculator must support additional services and parameter reuse without duplicating UI markup.

items.guidance: Use data attributes as JavaScript hooks and keep BEM classes presentation-only.
items.rationale: This keeps styling and behavior contracts independent.

entities.name: Configuration-Driven Calculator
entities.description: A calculator architecture where shared parameter definitions are combined with service-specific pricing, defaults, ordering, and allowed options.

items.guidance: Keep reusable parameter labels, option ids, option labels, and icons in parameter definitions, not service configs.
items.rationale: Service configs should only own service-specific pricing and availability.

items.guidance: Validate service configs before calculation and rendering.
items.rationale: Broken references, defaults, or modifiers should fail explicitly before reaching the UI.
